title: AppSec Event Handling and Blocking Flow
interaction_flow: AppSec Helper Daemon evaluates WAF rules against request data (headers, body, params)
dd_request_init returns: dd_success | dd_should_block | dd_should_redirect | dd_should_record | dd_network
dd_req_lifecycle_abort [user_req, begin/end stage] → dd_request_abort_static_page_spec(block_params, server) OR dd_request_abort_redirect_spec(block_params, server)
Blocking spec (status/headers/body) returned to framework → framework sends block response
_set_blocking_function validates _cur_req_span match, not already set, not after shutdown → ZVAL_COPY(&_blocking_function, blocking_function)
_call_blocking_function() → invokes stored PHP callable $blockingFunction(spec) via zend_call_function()
ddtrace ext → _response_commit(listener, span, status, resp_headers, rbe_zv)
_do_request_finish_user_req() → dd_request_shutdown(conn, &ctx) → AppSec Helper re-evaluates rules at response
_do_request_finish_user_req() → dd_tags_add_tags(_cur_req_span, superglob_equiv) → add security tags to span
Trigger path B: user_tracking.c._emit_user_event() [from track_user_login_success etc.]
ddtrace ext emits ASM (Application Security Management) event on current span
key_relationships: RequestLifecycle implements ddtrace_user_req_listeners interface (C struct with function pointers)
ddtrace.c bridges AppSec C extension → ddtrace PHP extension via dlsym symbol loading
dd_user_req_listeners registered at startup with priority=-10 (higher priority = earlier invocation)
SpanData/RootSpanData (PHP objects as zend_object*) passed through entire pipeline
block_params carries security_response_id + redirection_location for blocking decisions
_asm_event_emitted flag prevents duplicate ASM events per request

key_data_structures.ddtrace_user_req_listeners: C struct: { int priority; start_user_req*, response_committed*, finish_user_req*, set_blocking_function*, delete* }
key_data_structures.SpanData (PHP): name, service, resource, type, meta[], metrics[], peerServiceSources[], parent: SpanData|null, stack: SpanStack
key_data_structures.block_params: C struct: { zend_string* security_response_id; zend_string* redirection_location }

blocking_decision_matrix.user_req + REQUEST_BEGIN or REQUEST_END: Returns blocking spec (array with status/headers/body) to framework — framework sends response
blocking_decision_matrix.user_req + MID_REQUEST: Calls registered PHP $blockingFunction callable with spec
blocking_decision_matrix.non_user_req + REQUEST_END: Calls dd_trace_close_all_spans_and_flush() then dd_request_abort_static_page() or dd_request_abort_redirect() directly

important_notes: PHP namespace DDTrace\UserRequest provides the public API; actual logic is in C extension (request_lifecycle.c)
DDTrace\Testing\emit_asm_event() is the test/stub entry point; production path goes through user_tracking.c._emit_user_event()
has_listeners() in DDTrace\UserRequest returns bool — used by frameworks to skip overhead when AppSec not loaded
AppSec registers with ddtrace at startup via dd_trace_user_req_add_listeners; if ddtrace not loaded, AppSec operates in standalone PHP mode
The blocking function (set_blocking_function) is a PHP callable registered by the framework (e.g., Symfony/Laravel integration) to handle mid-request blocking gracefully (exception or early response)
ASM events are tied to SpanData on the root span; dd_tags_set_user_event_triggered() sets appsec.events.users.* tags
All blocking paths eventually produce a 'spec' array {status, headers, body} derived from WAF block_params
